section_miscategorizations.reason: Hepatomegaly is a clinical diagnosis/assessment term; a patient would not typically report this. It represents a clinician assessment of liver enlargement rather than a subjective symptom.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports an enlarged liver.
section_miscategorizations.reason: An enlarged liver is an objective clinical finding/diagnosis, not a typical patient-reported symptom. It aligns better with assessment of hepatomegaly rather than subjective history.

section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor feels a slight enlargement of the liver on the upper right side.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a physical exam finding (palpation of liver edge) and should be documented as an objective observation, not in the assessment section.

This could be interpreted as either an exam finding (objective) or a diagnostic statement (assessment). Given the context of palpation, it more closely reflects an exam finding and would fit better in O, but its presence in A is not strongly misleading.
This is an existing medication the patient is taking, which belongs in the subjective/history section unless framed as a new order or change.
